ground_truth.title: Online Class: Definition, Best Online Learning Platforms in 2025, and More! | Shorelight
ground_truth.author: Reyan Perumal
ground_truth.main_content: Online classes provide flexibility and convenience for students worldwide, and their popularity continues to rise each year. In 2024 alone, more than 26 million learners registered on Coursera, reflecting the growing demand for accessible, high-quality curricula that are accessible from anywhere in the world. Since the global shift to online learning during the COVID-19 pandemic, many colleges and universities have continued to offer online courses as part of their academic programs. An online class is a program or course of study that is delivered and accessed primarily through the internet rather than in a traditional physical classroom setting. An online class is conducted via a combination of live classes, recorded lectures, and downloadable reading materials, and students are assessed through interactive evaluations, online tests, and assignments. Online classes provide students more flexibility and control over how they want to approach learning in terms of pace and time while staying connected with classmates and instructors through digital platforms. The rise of online learning accelerated during the COVID-19 pandemic as universities temporarily closed their campuses to prevent the spread of the virus. Students and instructors adapted by shifting to virtual classrooms, and online user registrations on Coursera saw a threefold increase compared to 2016 with course enrollments nearly doubling from 2019 to 2020. In 2021 alone, enrollments rose by 32%, reaching 189 million. By 2024, enrollments on the platform reached more than 343 million, signaling continued interest in online education. The 8 best online learning platforms in 2025 include Udemy, Coursera, LinkedIn Learning, Harvard University Courses, Skillshare, edX, MasterClass, and Khan Academy. Online classes have much to offer students looking for flexible and affordable ways to study, including location independence, flexibility, cost-efficiency, 24/7 access, and personalized learning experiences. A digital classroom typically refers to a physical or hybrid learning environment where technology is fully integrated into teaching and learning, while a virtual classroom is a fully online learning environment that relies on network technology and virtual reality technology to achieve real-time interaction between teachers and students.
